reset_facts.text_preview:
input logic clk,
    input logic rst_n,
    input logic test_en_i,

    AXI_BUS.Slave slave[NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0],

.clk                    ( clk                ),
    .rst_n                  ( rst_n              ),
    .test_en_i              ( test_en_i          ),

    .slave_awid_i           ( s_slave_aw_id      ),
    .slave_awaddr_i         ( s_slave_aw_addr    ),

.slave_awsize_i         ( s_slave_aw_size    ),
    .slave_awburst_i        ( s_slave_aw_burst   ),
    .slave_awlock_i         ( s_slave_aw_lock    ),
    .slave_awcache_i        ( s_slave_aw_cache   ),
    .slave_awprot_i         ( s_slave_aw_prot    ),
    .slave_awregion_i       ( s_slave_aw_region  ),

.slave_arsize_i         ( s_slave_ar_size    ),
    .slave_arburst_i        ( s_slave_ar_burst   ),
    .slave_arlock_i         ( s_slave_ar_lock    ),
    .slave_arcache_i        ( s_slave_ar_cache   ),
    .slave_arprot_i         ( s_slave_ar_prot    ),
    .slave_arregion_i       ( s_slave_ar_region  ),

.master_awsize_o        ( s_master_aw_size   ),
    .master_awburst_o       ( s_master_aw_burst  ),
    .master_awlock_o        ( s_master_aw_lock   ),
    .master_awcache_o       ( s_master_aw_cache  ),
    .master_awprot_o        ( s_master_aw_prot   ),
    .master_awregion_o      ( s_master_aw_region ),

.master_arsize_o        ( s_master_ar_size   ),
    .master_arburst_o       ( s_master_ar_burst  ),
    .master_arlock_o        ( s_master_ar_lock   ),
    .master_arcache_o       ( s_master_ar_cache  ),
    .master_arprot_o        ( s_master_ar_prot   ),
    .master_arregion_o      ( s_master_ar_region ),

input  logic         clk_i,
   input  logic         rst_ni,
   input  logic         req_i,
   input  logic         we_i,
   input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
   input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,

always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
        if (~rst_ni) begin
            secure_reg <= mem;
        end else begin
        if(req_i) begin
            if (!we_i) begin

rstgen i_rstgen_main (
        .clk_i        ( clk_i                ),
        .rst_ni       ( rst_ni & (~ndmreset) ),
        .test_mode_i  ( test_en              ),
        .rst_no       ( ndmreset_n           ),

.clock                ( clk_i                ),
        .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
        .enable               ( jtag_enable[0]       ),
        .init_done            ( init_done            ),
        .jtag_TCK             ( jtag_TCK             ),
        .jtag_TMS             ( jtag_TMS             ),

.clk_i            ( clk_i           ),
        .rst_ni           ( rst_ni          ),
        .testmode_i       ( test_en         ),
        .jtag_key         ( jtag_key        ), 
        .dmi_req_o        ( jtag_dmi_req    ),
        .dmi_req_valid_o  ( jtag_req_valid  ),

.dmi_resp_valid_i ( jtag_resp_valid ),
        .dmi_rst_no       (                 ), // not connected
        .tck_i            ( jtag_TCK        ),
        .tms_i            ( jtag_TMS        ),
        .trst_ni          ( jtag_TRSTn      ),
        .td_i             ( jtag_TDI        ),

.tms_i            ( jtag_TMS        ),
        .trst_ni          ( jtag_TRSTn      ),
        .td_i             ( jtag_TDI        ),
        .td_o             ( jtag_TDO_data   ),
        .tdo_oe_o         ( jtag_TDO_driven ),
	.umode_o          ( ariane_umode    )

.clk                  ( clk_i                ),
            .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
            .debug_req_valid      ( dmi_req_valid        ),
            .debug_req_ready      ( debug_req_ready      ),
            .debug_req_bits_addr  ( dmi_req.addr         ),
            .debug_req_bits_op    ( debug_req_bits_op    ),

.clk_i                ( clk_i                ),
        .rst_ni               ( rst_ni               ), // PoR
        .testmode_i           ( test_en              ),
        .ndmreset_o           ( ndmreset             ),
        .dmactive_o           (                      ), // active debug session
        .debug_req_o          ( debug_req_core       ),

.testmode_i           ( test_en              ),
        .ndmreset_o           ( ndmreset             ),
        .dmactive_o           (                      ), // active debug session
        .debug_req_o          ( debug_req_core       ),
        .unavailable_i        ( '0                   ),
        .axi_s_req_i          ( dm_axi_s_req         ),

.axi_m_resp_i         ( dm_axi_m_resp        ),
        .dmi_rst_ni           ( rst_ni               ),
        .dmi_req_valid_i      ( debug_req_valid      ),
        .dmi_req_ready_o      ( debug_req_ready      ),
        .dmi_req_i            ( debug_req            ),
        .dmi_resp_valid_o     ( debug_resp_valid     ),

.clk_i  ( clk_i                   ),
        .rst_ni ( ndmreset_n              ),
        .slave  ( master[ariane_soc::ROM] ),
        .req_o  ( rom_req                 ),
        .we_o   ( rom_we                  ),
        .addr_o ( rom_addr                ),

.clk_i      ( clk_i                             ),
        .rst_ni     ( ndmreset_n                        ),
        .aw_valid_i ( master[ariane_soc::DRAM].aw_valid ),
        .aw_chan_i  ( aw_chan_i                         ),
        .aw_ready_o ( master[ariane_soc::DRAM].aw_ready ),
        .w_valid_i  ( master[ariane_soc::DRAM].w_valid  ),

.clk_i  ( clk_i      ),
        .rst_ni ( ndmreset_n ),
        .slave  ( dram       ),
        .req_o  ( req        ),
        .we_o   ( we         ),
        .addr_o ( addr       ),

.clk_i      ( clk_i                                                                       ),
        .rst_ni     ( rst_ni                                                                      ),
        .req_i      ( req                                                                         ),
        .we_i       ( we                                                                          ),
        .addr_i     ( addr[$clog2(NUM_WORDS)-1+$clog2(AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8):$clog2(AXI_DATA_WIDTH/8)] ),
        .wdata_i    ( wdata                                                                       ),

.clk          ( clk_i      ),
        .rst_n        ( ndmreset_n ),
        .test_en_i    ( test_en    ),
        .slave        ( slave      ),
        .master       ( master     ),
	.priv_lvl_i   ( priv_lvl   ),

.clk_i       ( clk_i          ),
        .rst_ni      ( ndmreset_n     ),
        .testmode_i  ( test_en        ),
        .axi_req_i   ( axi_clint_req  ),
        .axi_resp_o  ( axi_clint_resp ),
        .rtc_i       ( rtc_i          ),

.clk_i             ( clk_i                        ),
      .rst_ni            ( ndmreset_n                   ),
      .plic              ( master[ariane_soc::PLIC]     ),
      .uart              ( master[ariane_soc::UART]     ),
      .aes               ( master[ariane_soc::AES]      ), 
      .rom2_fuse         ( master[ariane_soc::ROM2]     ),

.eth_rxd           ( ),
      .eth_rst_n         ( ),
      .eth_tx_en         ( ),
      .eth_txd           ( ),
      .phy_mdio          ( ),
      .eth_mdc           ( ),

.clk_i                ( clk_i               ),
        .rst_ni               ( ndmreset_n          ),
        .boot_addr_i          ( ariane_soc::ROMBase ), // start fetching from ROM
        .hart_id_i            ( '0                  ),
        .irq_i                ( irqs                ),
        .ipi_i                ( ipi                 ),

csr_facts.text: * ROM2: Which have all the keys.

asset_candidates.label: ROM2 secure/readable memory contents
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: ROM/boot or secondary ROM storage is a protected asset candidate; contents can be read through rdata_o and updated through a write path.

asset_candidates.label: Boot ROM / ROM address regions
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Memory-mapped ROM regions are protected asset candidates because they may hold boot/security code or constants.

asset_candidates.label: Debug address region / debug module
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Debug capability and debug address space are protected asset/capability candidates.

asset_candidates.label: AXI fabric subordinate address map and access policy
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Fabric routing and connectivity/access-control policy determine which requester can reach peripherals and memory regions.

asset_candidates.label: AES peripheral address region
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: AES peripheral is a security-sensitive protected asset candidate.

asset_candidates.label: CLINT and PLIC control regions
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: Interrupt/timer control regions may affect privilege/control flow and are protected asset candidates.

asset_candidates.label: DRAM memory region
asset_candidates.permission_relevance: System memory region is a protected asset candidate depending on requester/privilege and address policy.

subject_candidates.label: CPU/core AXI master requester
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: AXI requester whose privilege level is supplied to fabric access-control logic.

subject_candidates.label: JTAG debug requester
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: External/test debug subject that can issue DMI/debug operations when selected.

subject_candidates.label: DMI/DTM debug requester
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: Debug module interface subject selected as alternate source to JTAG.

subject_candidates.label: SimJTAG / dmi_jtag / SimDTM instances
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: Testbench debug frontends that may drive debug/DMI requests.

subject_candidates.label: AXI node managers and subordinates
subject_candidates.permission_relevance: Bus participants involved in routing access requests and responses.

operation_candidates.label: ROM2 read
operation_candidates.operation: read/expose memory word via rdata_o when request is read and latched address is in range.

operation_candidates.label: ROM2 write/update
operation_candidates.operation: write/update secure_reg entry on requested write.

operation_candidates.label: AXI write transaction routing
operation_candidates.operation: route/configure write access from slave-side requester to selected master-side subordinate.

operation_candidates.label: AXI read transaction routing
operation_candidates.operation: route/expose read data from selected subordinate to requester.

operation_candidates.label: Debug request multiplexing
operation_candidates.operation: select JTAG or DMI debug request/response path.

guard_candidates.label: Privilege-level access mediation
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Connectivity is selected based on access-control bits indexed by privilege level.

guard_candidates.label: AXI address range decode
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Address ranges mediate which subordinate receives a transaction.

guard_candidates.label: AXI protection/user attributes
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: AXI protection/user attributes may carry privilege/security metadata used by downstream guards.

guard_candidates.label: AXI lock attributes
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Lock attributes may be relevant to mediated or exclusive accesses.

guard_candidates.label: Request/valid handshake gating
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Controls whether read/write/debug operations are performed, but is not by itself authorization.

guard_candidates.label: ROM2 bounds check on read data
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Read output is gated to zero when latched address is outside RomSize.

guard_candidates.label: Debug source selection
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Selects active debug frontend; may mediate which debug subject can drive the debug channel.

guard_candidates.label: Test enable disabled constant
guard_candidates.permission_relevance: Test enable is a lifecycle/test-mode guard candidate; in visible harness assigned inactive.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: ROM2 secure_reg reset/load state
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: On reset deassertion condition inverted in always_ff, secure_reg is loaded from mem on reset condition.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: ROM2 read address latch
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: raddr_q updates only on requested read; rdata_o reflects latched address.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: ROM2 write update state
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: secure_reg entry updates on requested write path.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: ROM2 out-of-range read output state
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: rdata_o returns zero when latched address is outside range.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Test mode lifecycle state
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: test_en is statically assigned inactive in the visible harness and passed into fabric/test-aware logic.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Debug frontend selection state
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: jtag_enable selects between JTAG-driven and DMI-driven debug request/response sources.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: DMI fallback inactive state
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: Visible assignments set non-JTAG DMI valid/op/exit to zero.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Privilege state propagation
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: Processor privilege level is assigned to priv_lvl and used by access-control/connectivity logic.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Access-control register slicing
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: Access-control register fields are sliced into per-manager/per-subordinate privilege permission vectors.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Reset/init transition
state_lifecycle_facts.lifecycle: init_done follows external reset; debug reset signals are present as lifecycle/control candidates.

path_candidates.label: AXI slave-side requester to master-side subordinate write path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Fabric path for writes to protected address regions/peripherals.

path_candidates.label: AXI subordinate read response to requester path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Fabric path exposing data from protected subordinate to requester.

path_candidates.label: Address range configuration into AXI routing
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Configured address ranges drive reachability of memory-mapped assets.

path_candidates.label: Privilege/access-control to connectivity map path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Permission decision path for fabric connectivity.

path_candidates.label: ROM2 read exposure path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Memory contents can be exposed on rdata_o through latched read address.

path_candidates.label: ROM2 write mutation path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Path by which external request signals can mutate ROM2 storage candidate.

path_candidates.label: JTAG to debug request path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: External debug frontend path into debug request channel.

path_candidates.label: DMI/SimDTM to debug request path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Alternate debug frontend path into debug request channel.

path_candidates.label: DRAM AXI channel path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Path connecting fabric DRAM port to DRAM memory interface.

path_candidates.label: Peripheral address-map path
path_candidates.permission_relevance: Address constants define software-visible paths to protected assets.

summary: Semantic labels identify ROM/debug/fabric/peripheral assets, CPU and debug requesters, read/write/debug routing operations, privilege/address/connectivity/test/debug guards, lifecycle states, and candidate reachability paths. No final vulnerability decision is made.
